channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Are Bigger AI Models Actually Making Developers Faster?
item.description: It's no secret that AI models have come a long way since the early days. They've become one of the most useful tools available to developers around the world. At this point, AI feels as essential...

item.title: Lessons from Embedding an LLM Inside a Drag-and-Drop Editor
item.description: When you integrate an AI generation step into a drag-and-drop editor, you face a problem that is rarely discussed in LLM tutorials: the model's output must be structurally valid, not just...

item.title: What Community Managers Know About First Users That Founders Forget
item.description: Disclosure: I do community work in the OpenNomos ecosystem, and 01MVP (https://01mvp.com) is one of the projects I help support. Opinions below are my own, learned the hard way. Founders talk...

item.title: Why I built realtime-calib: real-time, headless multi-camera calibration
item.description: I'm a freelance computer-vision developer with a PhD in human-movement science, and I build applied technology for health, physical activity and sports performance. This is the story of why I...

item.title: The status page you can't fake: measured uptime, not published
item.description: A status page is the one dashboard a company publishes about its own service. It is also the one place where the company has a reason to look good. That is a problem. If the page can be edited to...

item.title: The Trillion-Parameter RL Paper Is Really About Letting the Model Find the Workflow
item.description: The Trillion-Parameter RL Paper Is Really About Letting the Model Find the Workflow A new arXiv paper, Ring-Zero: Scaling Zero RL to a Trillion Parameters for Emergent Reasoning, reports a...

item.title: Why Not Every AI Application Needs Vector Embeddings
item.description: How building an AI chapter generator taught me to stop reaching for a vector database first. When I started building my AI chapter generator, I spent hours researching vector databases. I was...

item.title: The most complete Angular UI library has 35 open accessibility bugs. A fair teardown of PrimeNG on Angular 21.
item.description: PrimeNG calls itself "the most complete Angular UI component library," and points at Fortune 500s and governments as users. It's genuinely one of the most-used libraries in the Angular ecosystem —...

item.title: Ask HN: Is it just me, or is software buggier across the board?
item.description: I feel like I’m going crazy, I’ve been hitting the strangest bugs across all my devices in the last 6 months. Is anyone else experiencing this too? Comments URL:...

item.title: Your AI built something private. A public link is the wrong way to share it.
item.description: Building with AI got fast. You describe a thing, and a page, a dashboard, a small internal report, or a client-facing microsite comes out. The awkward part is the next step: you want exactly three...

item.title: Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
item.description: Mozilla has released updates to address two critical flaws in Firefox for which it warned that exploit code has been published. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-15718, an invalid...

item.title: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not models
item.description: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

item.title: Reelful’s AI turns your camera roll into short-form videos for social media
item.description: The app is designed for people who want to create social content, but find traditional video editing tools too complex or time-consuming.

item.title: Rime picks up $24M Series A to help enterprises field customer calls
item.description: Rime is handling over 100 million calls each month across multiple companies.

item.title: The tiniest MMO
item.description: At its peak, around 12 million people subscribed to World of Warcraft so that they could explore the realm of Azeroth together. The audience for PointlessQuest is quite a bit smaller. On launch...

item.title: The Motorola Edge 70 Max is all about power
item.description: Motorola has launched the Edge 70 Max, its latest flagship phone that's designed for power intensive tasks like streaming video and mobile gaming. Alongside having a huge battery and rapid wired...

item.title: The Download: a useful quantum machine and a record-breaking subsea tunnel
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer...

item.title: Indian AI coding startup Emergent becomes a unicorn with $130M Series C
item.description: The startup has reached a $120 million annualized revenue run rate and more than 200,000 paying customers.

item.title: Vint Cerf is working on a plan to unleash AI agents on the open internet
item.description: The guy behind TCP/IP is working on a standard for identifying AI agents in the wild.

item.title: An Inventor of Apple's FaceID Wants to Analyze Your Brain's Health With AI
item.description: Gidi Littwin's new AI startup, Hemispheric, makes diagnostic brain scans for conditions like depression, PTSD, and Parkinson’s. He wants the technology to be as cheap and easy as a blood test.

item.title: Home Depot’s viral 12-foot skeleton now talks
item.description: The Home Depot is once again upgrading its 12-foot-tall skeleton to help keep the viral piece of Halloween decor popular as spooky season creeps closer. Skelly is borrowing some of the tech...

item.title: SASE Has An AI Blind Spot. Inspecting Packets Is No Longer Enough.
item.description: For years, routing traffic through cloud proxies was good enough. Then work moved to the browser, AI entered the workflow, and the inspection model stopped keeping up. Enterprise workflows now...

item.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
item.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive. It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary...

item.title: New Webinar: Closing the Approval Gap in AI-Era Ad Tech
item.description: A single approved marketing tag can quietly load fourth-party code your security team has never seen, granting full access to your forms, customer data, and checkout pages. This on-demand webinar...

item.title: A Video Screen That Is Also a Camera
item.description: Amazing: Researchers from ETH Zurich in Switzerland, however, managed to create a new type of pixel that can simultaneously do both. This hypercharged pixel, called a Fourier pixel, can generate...

item.title: Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windows Code Execution
item.description: Open a repository in Cursor on Windows and, if a file named git.exe is sitting in the project root, Cursor runs it. No click, no approval dialog, no warning that anything in the folder is about to...

item.title: Samsung’s new foldable display is harder to crease and damage
item.description: Samsung has unveiled a new flexible display technology for foldable phones that's designed to be slimmer, more durable, and less prone to creasing. The Flex Titanium tech is the culmination of...

item.title: Spotify’s Daniel Ek is bringing his body-scanning clinics to the US
item.description: Spotify founder Daniel Ek's body-scanning startup, Neko Health, is setting its sights on the United States after raising $700 million from a star-studded group of celebrities, entrepreneurs, and...

item.title: My Ebike Delivery Went Missing. When I Tried to Recover It, I Ended Up in Chatbot Hell
item.description: Companies’ increasing reliance on AI chatbots isn’t making the customer service experience smarter. It’s just making it more infuriating.

item.title: The PS6 sure sounds like a handheld
item.description: The video game industry is in turmoil. Microsoft and Sony are starting to pivot to their next consoles, but it's not looking great: Prices are soaring, Sony is killing the video game disc, and...
